title: S-Meas well-founded-descent contract
description: Specifies the well-founded measure used to prove termination of any loop, peeling process, refinement, repair attempt, or repeated routing cycle, computed from recorded branch-state data and strictly decreasing on non-closing passes. The record operationalizes the S-Meas well-founded-descent lemma schema in the structural exhaustion framework (branch_closure_methodology_extended.tex, "Lemma schemas").

schema.description: Required contract discriminator; it must be the literal `S-Meas`.

measure.description: Required definition of the quantity or tuple used to prove progress, such as a lexicographic state size, unresolved payload count, remaining tactic queue length, rank quotient, budget tier, finite-table index, or other measure derived from B.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose definition of the measure, including its domain and every tuple coordinate.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX formula for the measure.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used for the measure.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable definition of the measure.

sourceData.description: Required nonempty list of recorded state fields from which the measure is computed. It must contain all inputs and must not depend on future choices or unrecorded proof-search state.

items.description: One precise state-field path or canonical object used to evaluate the measure.

wellFoundedCodomain.description: Required definition of the ordered codomain and proof that it is well founded, such as a natural-number order, lexicographic product, multiset extension, finite tactic-state order, or declared progress order from the branch state.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose description of the carrier set, strict order, and well-foundedness fact.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX notation for the codomain and order.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used for the order.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable definition and well-foundedness theorem for the codomain.

strictDecrease.description: Required theorem that every non-closing pass maps an admitted source state to a destination state with strictly smaller measure in `wellFoundedCodomain`; non-increase is insufficient.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose progress theorem, including the non-closing premise and quantified transition.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX inequality or order relation expressing strict descent.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used for the descent proof.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable theorem proving strict decrease on every non-closing transition.

nonClosingPassRef.description: Optional stable identifier of the transition, loop body, or branch predicate to which the strict-decrease theorem applies; provide it whenever more than one pass or transition could otherwise be meant.

evidence.description: Required nonempty list of sources validating the measure definition, well-founded order, and strict-decrease proof.

kind.description: Required evidence category from the fixed evidence-kind alphabet.

reference.description: Required nonempty locator for the measure definition, S-Meas record, minimality/replacement argument, loop transition, S-Rest proof, progress-order proof, or formal well-foundedness theorem.

hash.description: Optional integrity hash for a program or formal artifact that computes or verifies the measure.

notes.description: Optional note identifying whether the source establishes definability, well-foundedness, or strict descent.
